seo.title: AI and the Law | Working Group 13
seo.description: A modern React rebuild of The Sedona Conference Working Group 13 page for AI and the Law.

branding.title: AI and the Law
branding.subtitle: The Sedona Conference working group page

hero.title: AI and the Law
hero.description: Consistent with The Sedona Conference mission of moving the law forward in a reasoned and just way, WG13 frames the legal issues presented by AI, analyzes how AI affects law and legal practice, and articulates principles and best practices for AI development and implementation.

items.label: Next meeting
items.description: The Annual Meeting 2026 is the next published WG13 event and the clearest date members should plan around.

items.label: Active work
items.description: Definitions, AI governance, the regulatory crosswalk, and the custom Sedona AI initiative are all active right now.

items.label: Publication status
items.description: The current WG13 page does not show a publication open for public comment yet, so meetings and drafting tracks are the clearest signals of what is active.

activities.title: Current Activities
activities.description: The original content is strong; the main issue is presentation. A card-based structure makes each drafting effort scannable without losing detail.

items.title: Consensus Definitions Relating to AI and the Law
items.description: The drafting team is developing consensus definitions for terms at the intersection of AI and legal practice.

items.title: How-To Guide for AI Governance
items.description: This workstream combines principles, implementation practices, and planning for future regulation into actionable guidance.

items.title: AI and the Law Regulatory Crosswalk
items.description: The crosswalk maps where current laws already address AI concerns and where targeted amendments or new legislation may be needed.

items.title: Develop and Document Custom Sedona AI
items.description: This initiative explores a Sedona AI tool built with LLM and RAG patterns while documenting the product-development process for the legal community.

meetings.title: Meetings
meetings.description: Separating the next scheduled gathering from the archive makes the page feel active and helps members find the right next action quickly.

meetings.title: The Sedona Conference Working Group 13 Annual Meeting 2026
meetings.date: Thursday, Apr 09, 2026 - 08:30 am to Friday, Apr 10, 2026 - 02:00 pm

tracks.title: Past Meetings
tracks.summary: Recent meetings preserved as a compact archive.

meetings.title: The Sedona Conference Working Group 13 Midyear Meeting 2025
meetings.date: Thursday, Sep 11, 2025 - 08:45 am to Friday, Sep 12, 2025 - 01:00 pm

meetings.title: The Sedona Conference Working Group 13 on Artificial Intelligence and the Law: Inaugural Meeting 2025
meetings.date: Thursday, Jan 16, 2025 - 08:30 am to Friday, Jan 17, 2025 - 03:00 pm

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: A modern build can treat empty content, restricted content, and evergreen links differently instead of rendering each one with the same generic Drupal shell.

cards.title: Members-only workspace
cards.description: Working group materials, announcements, and draft-comment notices are available after sign-in for active members.

cards.title: Publication open for public comment
cards.description: No WG13 publication is currently open for public comment. The live page remains the best place to monitor the next release.

cards.title: Updates and announcements
cards.description: The exported page shows no current updates or announcements, which makes this a good candidate for a cleaner empty state.

cards.title: Publications
cards.description: The original page exposes a publications section but no rendered cards, so this version turns it into a clear callout instead of a blank block.

cards.title: Reference library
cards.description: Reference materials are for educational use by working group members and are not intended to be copied or republished.

aside.title: Members-only content can be modeled cleanly in React
aside.description: Instead of embedding login reminders inside a generic content block, this page pulls access guidance into a focused panel with direct actions.

committee.title: Steering Committee
committee.description: Breaking the roster into role-based cards keeps the hierarchy clear and gives each profile link proper breathing room.

footer.description: The Sedona Conference is a nonpartisan, nonprofit research and educational institute dedicated to advancing law and policy through consensus-driven publications and legal education.
